title: trochera barbinodis
side_effects: Trochera barbinodis can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile oils can cause headaches, dizziness, and even respiratory problems in some individuals. In severe cases, improper use of Trochera barbinodis can lead to liver and kidney damage, as well as an increased risk of seizures and other neurological disorders. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using this plant for any health purposes to avoid these potential side effects.
